issue_title: Map storage looks up ID via the session, that is no longer needed
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The following example first looks up the realmId, then looks it up via the session again. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fc974fc01911af06e8431f03a0389a69a0131473/model/map/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/role/MapRoleProvider.java#L194-L200

Such a behavior will lead to problem in a tree store, where a partial store should return only its partial results.

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

As no caches are in place outside of the map store, this behavior is now no longer needed. All legacy caches are disabled.

### Actual behavior

Additional lookup by ID
commit_message:
Use session level cache and avoid resolving by ID too often

Closes #12381

issue_title: JPA map store uses bulk delete, but keeps entities in the persistence context
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The current bulk delete doesn't clear or flush which is normally used before or after a bulk operation. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/be28e866b9bdfac39cae13d06a010994a1e058a7/model/map-jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/jpa/JpaMapKeycloakTransaction.java#L149-L162

With the JPA map store implementation a clear would detach all entities in the session, what is not intended here. Without the clear, Hibernate will only flush changes to the DB (as it sees that the modified entity is in a table that is being touched by the query), but will still keep the entity earmarked for an optimistic lock update at the end of the transaction to ensure that it hasn't been modified.


### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

After a bulk delete, the entities that are in the persistence context and that have been deleted by the bulk operation should also be marked as deleted in the persistence context.

### Actual behavior

Entities are still in the persistence context and might trigger a optimistic lock exception on commit.

### How to Reproduce?

Seen in #12241
commit_message:
Keep a list of model instances in the JPA map session.

This allows removing them from the persistence context on bulk delete.

Closes #12384

issue_title: KC_DB_USERNAME and KC_DB_PASSWORD are not respected when properties are set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When `db-username` and `db-password` properties are defined in the `/opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf` file and `KC_DB_USERNAME` and `KC_DB_PASSWORD` environment variables are set, Keycloak does not respect these environment variables and uses values from the file. However args `--db-username` and `--db-password` override properties from the file.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

`KC_DB_USERNAME` and `KC_DB_PASSWORD` environment variables should override `db-username` and `db-password` properties defined in the `/opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf` file

### Actual behavior

`db-username` and `db-password` properties are used instead of respectful environment variables.

### How to Reproduce?

Dockerfile:
```
ARG BASE_IMAGE_PATH
ARG BASE_IMAGE_VERSION

FROM ${BASE_IMAGE_PATH}:${BASE_IMAGE_VERSION}

ENV KC_DB=postgres

COPY conf/ /opt/keycloak/conf/
RUN /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build

```
conf/keycloak.conf file:
```
# Database settings
db=postgres
db-url-host=postgres
db-url-port=5432
db-url-database=keycloak
db-schema=public
db-username=keycloak
db-password=password

# Cache settings
cache=local

# Hostname settings
hostname-strict=false

# HTTP settings
http-enabled=true
http-relative-path=auth

# Proxy mode
proxy=edge

# Metrics and healthcheck settings
metrics-enabled=true
health-enabled=true

# Enabled features
features.admin_fine_grained_authz=enabled
features.client_secret_rotation=enabled
features.token_exchange=enabled

# Logging configuration
log-level=info
log-console-color=true

# Override for local development mode
%dev.spi-theme-cache-themes=false
%dev.spi-theme-cache-templates=false
%dev.spi-theme-static-max-age=-1

# Logging configuration. INFO is the default level for most of the categories
#quarkus.log.level = DEBUG
quarkus.log.category."org.jboss.resteasy.resteasy_jaxrs.i18n".level=WARN
quarkus.log.category."org.infinispan.transaction.lookup.JBossStandaloneJTAManagerLookup".level=WARN
quarkus.log.sentry=true
quarkus.log.sentry.dsn=https://sentry
quarkus.log.sentry.in-app-packages=*
quarkus.log.sentry.level=WARN
quarkus.log.sentry.server-name=keycloak
quarkus.log.sentry.release=18.0.0-iponweb-10.0
quarkus.log.sentry.environment=dev
```
Build an image:
```
docker build --build-arg BASE_IMAGE_PATH=quay.io/keycloak/keycloak --build-arg BASE_IMAGE_VERSION=18.0.0 . -t keycloak_test
```
Run the container with valid DB credentials:
```
docker run --rm -e KC_DB_USERNAME=keycloak_test -e KC_DB_PASSWORD="<PASSWORD>" -e KC_DB_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/keycloak_x_test keycloak_test --verbose start
```
Logs:

It seems that Quarkus mixes tables from the two databases when Hibernate loads during startup and the "Realm" table overrides the default one ("REALM").
The strange thing is that sometimes no error appears during bootstrap, maybe because Keycloak entities classes were loaded first?

After a lot of investigations, I found that this error is not related to the UserStorageProvider implementation but is caused by the presence of a Realm class into the persistence.xml file.

I made a really simple demo with only a persistence.xml file embedded in a jar, with only a Realm class. No UserStorageProvider is needed to reproduce this error.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Using an external database with a table already defined in Keycloak does not throw an exception at startup, like in the Wildfly distribution.

### Actual behavior

Sometimes server does not start because the table Realm cannot be found by Hibernate.

### How to Reproduce?

Update keycloak.conf as follow:
```
db=mariadb
db-username=keycloak
db-password=keycloak
db-url=jdbc:mariadb://127.0.0.1:3306/keycloak
```

Add a quarkus.properties file with:
```
quarkus.datasource.user-store.db-kind=mariadb
quarkus.datasource.user-store.username=sa
quarkus.datasource.user-store.password=sa
quarkus.datasource.user-store.jdbc.url=jdbc:mariadb://127.0.0.1:3306/external_db
```

Create two empty databases "keycloak" and "external_db" in your MariaDB instance and also the corresponding users.

Copy the jar from the tar.gz archive attached into the providers directory and launch Keycloak with command:
`./kc.sh build && ./kc.sh start-dev --transaction-xa-enabled=false`

Note : it may require multiple launches to display the error.

[user-storage-jpa-realm.tar.gz](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/8870822/user-storage-jpa-realm.tar.gz)

issue_title: Authorization Permission Mismatch in RPT and Permission Ticket
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I was playing around with the resources feature in Keycloak and noticed a strange behavior when evaluating the authorization permissions in the access tokens of resources owned by a user other than the client itself.
While requesting an access token with grant type `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket` (using an access token obtained with direct grant), the permission to a resource owned by another user seem to not be granted.
However, when requesting a similar access token, but this time using a previously acquired permission ticket, the permissions seem to be granted.

Using the `evaluate` feature of the admin console, I noticed that the permissions obtained using the permission ticket match the ones evaluated in the admin console, while the other access token does not contain the expected permissions.

While I can explicitly request permission using the `permission` parameter in the request, I'm wondering why the resource is not included in the token by default, even though I have access?
Moreover, when creating the resource as owned by the client (and not another user), it is included in the token by default.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

I'd expect the first access token to contain the same permissions as the second access token obtained using a permission ticket.

### Actual behavior

The permissions differ.

### How to Reproduce?

Make sure to have at least 2 users in your realm.

1. Create a new client.
    - Enable Direct Access Grants
    - Enable Service Accounts
    - Enable Authorization
2. Create a new resource owned by user1 (this can only be done through a request, at least I didn't find a way to specify a different owner than the client itself in the admin console).
   - Make sure it has some scopes assigned that we can request later on
   - Also it must have the `urn:CLIENT_ID:resources:default` type
4. Obtain the first access token
   1. Obtain an access token for user2 using direct grant
   2. Exchange the access token for a `uma-ticket` using the `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket` grant type.
   3. Decode the resulting token and note the authorization permissions. They don't contain the permissions for the newly created resource.
5. Obtain the second access token
  1. Obtain an access token for user2 using direct grant
  2. Exchange the direct grant for a permission ticket requesting the assigned resource scopes
  3. Decode the resulting token and note the authorization permissions again. This time, they contain the permissions for the newly created resource.

You may also adjust and use the following script to reproduce the steps above.
Note that it requires `jq` and only works as long as the resource has not been created already (as we don't get the ID otherwise).

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# base address
KC_BASE="http://localhost:8080/auth"
# realm
KC_REALM="realm"
# id of user1, the owner of the resource
KC_OWNER_ID="3019fc73-1247-4df3-ad07-298e6d66a517"
# username of user2, the one requesting access
KC_USERNAME="username"
# password of user2
KC_PASSWORD="password"
# client id of resource client
KC_CLIENT="client"
# client secret of resource client
KC_SECRET="secret"

resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
 -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
 -d "grant_type=password" \
 -d "client_id=$KC_CLIENT" -d "client_secret=$KC_SECRET" \
 -d "username=$KC_USERNAME" -d "password=$KC_PASSWORD")
userToken=$(echo $resp | jq -r .access_token)

resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
 -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
 -d "grant_type=client_credentials" \
 -d "client_id=$KC_CLIENT" -d "client_secret=$KC_SECRET")
clientToken=$(echo $resp | jq -r .access_token)

# Create resource
resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/authz/protection/resource_set" \
 -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 -H "Authorization: Bearer $clientToken" \
 --data "{\"name\": \"demo\",\"owner\": {\"id\": \"$KC_OWNER_ID\"},\"scopes\": [{\"name\": \"read\"}],\"type\": \"urn:$KC_CLIENT:resources:default\"}"
)
echo "Created resource: $resp"

resource_id=$(echo $resp | jq -r ._id)

# Get permissions using RPT
resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
 -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
 -H "Authorization: Bearer $userToken" \
 -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket" \
 -d "response_include_resource_name=true" \
 -d "audience=$KC_CLIENT" )

rpt=$(echo $resp | jq -r .access_token)

echo "Permissions from RPT:"
echo $rpt | jq -R 'split(".") | .[0],.[1] | @base64d | fromjson | .authorization.permissions'

# Get permissions using permission ticket
resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/authz/protection/permission" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $userToken" \
  --data "[{\"resource_id\": \"$resource_id\",\"resource_scopes\": [\"read\"]}]" )
ticket=$(echo $resp | jq -r .ticket)

resp=$(curl -s -X POST "$KC_BASE/realms/$KC_REALM/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $userToken" \
  -d "audience=$KC_CLIENT" \
  -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket" \
  -d "response_include_resource_name=true" \
  -d "ticket=$ticket")
rpt2=$(echo $resp | jq -r .access_token)

echo "Permissions using Ticket:"
echo $rpt2 | jq -R 'split(".") | .[0],.[1] | @base64d | fromjson | .authorization.permissions'
```

As a result, I get the following output. The first token does not contain the permission for the `demo` resource, while the second token obtained using a permission ticket contains it.

```
Permissions from RPT:
null
[
  {
    "rsid": "2e069564-07c0-490e-97f9-e2f354784b06",
    "rsname": "Default Resource"
  }
]
Permissions using Ticket:
null
[
  {
    "scopes": [
      "read"
    ],
    "rsid": "cdfd4975-0678-4b4a-92cb-e92c67b64699",
    "rsname": "demo"
  }
]
```

issue_title: AuthzClient fails if it can not access the frontend-url
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using the AuthzClient in a backend server, which can only access the keycloak through the backend-url not the frontend-url fails. 

The problem is, that the .well-known/uma2-configuration returns the frontend-urls for the "policy_endpoint" and the "permission_endpoint".

We run into the problem when using quarkus integration tests, where the keycloak is run inside the same docker-network as the docker-container under test. So the quarkus-application can access the keycloak only through the backend-url, whereas the application running the unit-tests can only access through the front url, the port bound to localhost.

There should either be a possibility to get the uma2-configuration with backend-urls. Possibly return backend-url endpoints when accessing through the backend-url and return frontend-url when accessing through the frontend-url?

Or there should be a possibility to override the urls for the AuthzClient instead of using the well-known endpoint.

18.0.0

The AuthzClient should also be working with backend-urls only

The AuthzClient fails if it has no access to the frontend-urls

* Configur a frontendurl for the 
* Call the https://keyclaok-backend-url/realms/{relam}/.well-known/uma2-configuration., then you get the "policy_endpoint" and as a frontent-url.

issue_title: Keycloak accepts a client scope name that violates OAuth2.0 scope-token definition rule
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Keycloak accepts a client scope name that violates the OAuth2.0 scope-token definition rule.

OAuth2.0 specification defines scope and scope-token format in this way in ABNF notation,
```
     scope       = scope-token *( SP scope-token )
     scope-token = 1*( %x21 / %x23-5B / %x5D-7E )
```
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-3.3

but Keycloak accepts "(double-quote) or multi-bytes characters.
This may break interoperability with other OAuth2 implementations.


### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

client scope name should be validated against the OAuth2.0 specification rule during creation.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Create a client scope with a name "マルチバイト文字(multi bytes characters)" from the Admin console.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

In the case where a user does not belong to any groups, the following two places would result in `org.hibernate.exception.SQLGrammarException`.

issue_title: False implementation of SAML element EncryptionMethod
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Schema Definition of [SAML element EncryptionMethod](https://www.w3.org/TR/2002/REC-xmlenc-core-20021210/Overview.html#sec-EncryptionMethod) is : 

>   <complexType name='EncryptionMethodType' mixed='true'>
    <sequence>
      <element name='KeySize' minOccurs='0' type='xenc:KeySizeType'/>
      <element name='OAEPparams' minOccurs='0' type='base64Binary'/>
      <any namespace='##other' minOccurs='0' maxOccurs='unbounded'/>
    </sequence>
    <attribute name='Algorithm' type='anyURI' use='required'/>
  </complexType>

Keycloak only honors and parses KeySize and OAEPparams. If an EncryptionMethod exists with any other valid xml element, Keycloak throws a RuntimeException. Due to it, valid xml files can not be parsed and inserted as SAML Identity Provider or SAML Client. Examples can be found to https://md.aai.grnet.gr/feeds/edugain-samlmd.xml ( fe SP with entity id 'https://sam.ihsmarkit.com/sso/Ukfed-W228SA1MKb' ).

EncryptionMethod must be enhanced with a List<Object> any and SAMLEncryptionMethodParser must add in this least all xml elements expect from KeySize and OAEPparams.


### Version

16.1

### Expected behavior

Xml element must be parsed as any elemnt

### Actual behavior

A RuntimeExceptio is thrown.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to add entity with entity id 'https://sam.ihsmarkit.com/sso/Ukfed-W228SA1MKb' from https://md.aai.grnet.gr/feeds/edugain-samlmd.xml as SAML Client.

### Anything else?

_No response_

SAML element EncryptionMethod can consist any element

closes #12585

issue_title: Read/write configuration for user profile also for username/email not possible
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

To allow keycloak to run as pure ID broker (no IDP functionality), we must be able to forbid the user from changing attributes coming from an external IDP.

The user profile does allow to configure this on a per-attribute basis, however this does currently not work for username and  email.


### Version

18.0

### Expected behavior

The administrator can configure permissions to all attributes (including username and email) in the user profile configuration UI.
This configuration is handled correctly by the Account API.

### Actual behavior

It's not possible to configure permissions on username and email attributes in the user profile configuration UI.
(The "Permissions" desctions is not available for these attributes.)

### How to Reproduce?

Enable declarative user profile by setting -Dkeycloak.profile.feature.declarative_user_profile=enabled.

* got to "Realm settings" and check "User Profile Enabled"
* switch to the "User Profile" tab and press the "Create" button to create a new attribute
* enter "username" or "email" as attribute name
* the "Permissions" section disappears in the moment the attribute name has been entered completely

### Anything else?

I'll provide a fix for this
commit_message:
Allow permission configuration for username and email in user profile.
Enhanced Account API to respect access to these attributes.

Resolves #12599

issue_title: CVE-2022-23221 - H2 vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via a jdbc:h2:mem JDBC URL
issue_body:
## Summary

[com.h2database:h2](http://www.h2database.com/html/main.html) is a database engine

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via a jdbc:h2:mem JDBC URL containing the `IGNORE_UNKNOWN_SETTINGS=TRUE;FORBID_CREATION=FALSE;INIT=RUNSCRIPT` substring.

## Version

18.0.0 or higher

## Impact

Low. Keycloak is not vulnerable to this issue. 

## Remediation

Upgrade `com.h2database:h2` to version 2.1.210 or higher.

## References

-   [GitHub Release](https://github.com/h2database/h2database/releases/tag/version-2.1.210)
-   [PoC](https://packetstormsecurity.com/files/165676/H2-Database-Console-Remote-Code-Execution.html)
-   [Twitter](https://twitter.com/d0nkey_man/status/1483824727936450564)

## Credits

* https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMH2DATABASE-2348247

## Additional information

Keycloak is **not** vulnerable, but it is affected considering the usage of _h2database_ dependency in the WildFly legacy and Quarkus distributions.
commit_message:
Updating H2 database to 2.x

Closes #12607

Co-authored-by: Stian Thorgersen <stian@redhat.com>

issue_title: Some tests are failing with `No tests matching pattern "..." were executed!`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

This is caused by updating Surefire in #12359.

Even though it was not stated in the [release notes](https://blogs.apache.org/maven/entry/apache-maven-surefire-failsafe-plugin), the behaviour of Surefire's `failIfNoSpecifiedTests` property was actually changed in `3.0.0-M6` and [now defaults to `true`](https://github.com/apache/maven-surefire/pull/350/files#diff-051d2dce875d49a0a454833dd3b5ad343904566e66f8ab945dc333bba095c3e0R121). This caused our test runs to be failing in some cases, specifically when we are running a Maven test build across multiple modules together with the `-Dtest=...` property. That is because the `-Dtest=...` filter was not satisfied with some of the modules and due to `failIfNoSpecifiedTests` newly set to `true` the build failed.

This was partially mitigated in GHA by separating building the tests dependencies and tests itself:
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12359/commits/e96b81e99419e9f2b376d3b4a5f8ba32cf65d756#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fR439
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12584/commits/073d90c45a5a7b5c341de0028f2a613c0194f8a8

But that is not a correct solution as it doesn't solve to root cause and some tests were still affected, see e.g. [WebAuthn tests](https://github.com/vmuzikar/keycloak/runs/6971002220?check_suite_focus=true).

**Proposed solution:**
* Explicitly set `failIfNoSpecifiedTests` to `false` across the project to keep the old behaviour.
* Revert changes to GHA from #12359 and #12584 as they are no longer necessary.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message: Explicitly set `failIfNoSpecifiedTests` to `false`

issue_title: GET /{realm}/users/{id}/groups ignores 'search' query parameter
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The search parameter gets ignored completely, it always just returns the groups the user is a member of, without filtering them with the search parameter.

### Version

15.1.1

If you provide the search query parameter, the endpoint should filter group names using the search query.

### Actual behavior

When you provide a search parameter that doesn't match any group name (or anything else for that matter) it still returns all of the users groups.

### How to Reproduce?

Send a valid http request: GET /{realm}/users/{id}/groups
It will return the useres groups.
Now send the same request with an invalid search appended => GET /{realm}/users/{id}/groups?search=blablablabla
It will still return the same groups altho none of them match "blablablabla"

### Anything else?

https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/15.1/rest-api/index.html#_users_resource
search for -> GET /{realm}/users/{id}/groups

### How to Reproduce?

I think I've reduced this to the minimum number of steps in order to reproduce this problem.  Removing any of the steps will not reproduce the issue.

Start the server, add a client 

Running the following block of requests in multiple threads will cause the error:

1. Get a client token via POST request to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/realms/[realm]/protocol/openid-connect/token
2. Create a unique user via POST request to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/admin/realms/[realm]/users
3. Create a unique resource in the test client via POST request to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/realms/[realm]/authz/protection/resource_set
4. Create a unique policy for that resource and user with at least one authorization scope via POST request to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/realms/[realm]/authz/protection/uma-policy/[resource-id]
5. Delete the policy via DELETE request to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/realms/[realm]/authz/protection/uma-policy/[policy-id]
6. Get a list of existing policies via GET to http://[host]:[port]/[path]/realms/[realm]/authz/protection/uma-policy?first=0&max=500

Step #6 is what will generate the error (again, only during concurrent requests).  Also, if you replace user in the above steps with a role then you will get a similar error but with the RolePolicyProviderFactory instead of UserPolicyProviderFactory.


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CWE-295 - Improper Certificate Validation vulnerability in io.netty:netty-handler
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

[io.netty:netty-handler](https://github.com/netty/netty.git/netty-handler) is a library that provides an asynchronous event-driven network application framework and tools for rapid development of maintainable high performance and high scalability protocol servers and clients. In other words, Netty is a NIO client server framework which enables quick and easy development of network applications such as protocol servers and clients. It greatly simplifies and streamlines network programming such as TCP and UDP socket server.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Certificate Validation. Certificate hostname validation is disabled by default in `Netty 4.1.x` which makes it potentially susceptible to Man-in-the-Middle attacks.

## Version

<= 18.0.0

## Impact

- Pending  

## Remediation

There is no fixed version for `io.netty:netty-handler`. Possible mitigation was suggested here [https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/8537#issuecomment-822441268](https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/8537#issuecomment-822441268)

## References

-   [GitHub Issue 1](https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/10806)
-   [GitHub Issue 2](https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/8537)
-   [GitHub Issue 3](https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/9930)
-   [GitHub Issue 4](https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/10362)

## Credits

issue_title: Slow user query by attribute
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If querying users by attribute values the response is really slow. 

On further investigation the query is missing the database index because attribute name and value is queried by lower value, eg. lower(name) and lower(value).

Either remove lower and match the actual value (which seems to be the more reasonable approach) or add a matching new index to the attribute table: 
``` CREATE INDEX index ON user_attribute(lower(name), lower(value)); ```

### Version

18.0.1

### Expected behavior

Query by attribute returns in a reasonable time.

### Actual behavior

Query takes really long and produces a lot of load on the database.

### How to Reproduce?

Just query by attribute.

### Anything else?

Adding a max value to the query totally breaks the request. (Timeouts)

issue_title: OIDC logout fails without id_token_hint
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to logout from Keycloak with a logout uri just containing the post_logout_redirect_uri param - without id_token_hint param - the following error is shown: "Missing parameters: id_token_hint".

I would expect to see a logout confirmation screen instead. Btw, that's the way it is done in "legacy" mode (property spi-login-protocol-openid-connect-legacy-logout-redirect-uri=true), when just redirect_uri is specified.

**This is a critical bug, because the logout for several of our customer applications does no longer work with Keycloak 18.0.1 - the logout worked without id_token_hint in Keycloak 17.0.1.**

Showing a logout confirmation screen would conform to the OIDC spec, which states [1]:
"An id_token_hint is also RECOMMENDED when this parameter is included." (It's RECOMMENDED, *not* REQUIRED)
...
"At the Logout Endpoint, the OP SHOULD ask the End-User whether to log out of the OP as well. Furthermore, the OP MUST ask the End-User this question if an id_token_hint was not provided or if the supplied ID Token does not belong to the current OP session with the RP and/or currently logged in End-User. If the End-User says "yes", then the OP MUST log out the End-User."


[1] https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-rpinitiated-1_0.html

### Version

18.0.1

### Expected behavior

In case a post_logout_redirect_uri is provided, but no id_token_hint is provided, a logout confirmation screen should be shown.

### Actual behavior

An error occurs: "Missing parameters: id_token_hint".

### How to Reproduce?

Use Keycloak 18.0.1 with default configuration.

Open the following URI:
http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?post_logout_redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fadmin%2Fmaster%2Fconsole%2F%23%2Fserver-info

The following error page with the following content shown:

> We are sorry...
> Missing parameters: id_token_hint


### Anything else?

If you confirm that this is a bug, I would be happy to provide a PR fixing it.

issue_title: Cannot use WebAuthn with WildFly distribution
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

It's not possible to use the WebAuthn component with the WildFly distribution ATM. It seems it's caused by some inaccuracies with transitive dependencies included in some particular WildFly dependencies. 

I've noticed the required `CBORFactory._createContentReference` method with the particular set of parameters was added to the Jackson dependency since the 2.13 version. However, the stack trace of the error in the log shows that Jackson `2.12.6.1` is used. I tried to analyze those transitive dependencies with `mvn dependency:tree` but didn't spot any place where the `2.12.6.1` version is used.

When I use Undertow (`auth-server-undertow`) or Quarkus (`auth-server-quarkus`) auth server, everything works as expected.

--------------------------------------------
I've also noticed the `server-galleon-pack` contains, after build, in the target directory `jackson-databind` module, where the version of Jackson databind `2.12.6.1` is used. Maybe, it's an entry point to it.
